content.claim: International comparative literature finds that earlier age of selection and greater number of school-types are significantly associated with higher inequality of opportunity; Hungary's simultaneous operation of 4-, 6-, and 8-year admission tracks is described as very rare internationally, existing mainly in Czech Republic and partly in Slovakia.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Comparative studies establish correlation between early selection and inequality, not mechanism of causation specific to Hungary
The rarity finding comes from a single sociological source, not systematically surveyed across jurisdictions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
